results.title: Thread Detective
results.instruction: Read the four scheduling threads from Christopher Becker. The proposed meeting times are 9:00 AM, 11:00 AM, 2:30 PM, and 4:00 PM. Another email from Janice Carlson (subject: 'Calendar conflicts for the Atlas launch review') shows which of those times conflict with the calendar. Reply to Christopher Becker's most recent scheduling thread confirming the one time that does not conflict.

criteria_results.check: Reply contains the correct non-conflicting time
criteria_results.expr: '4:00 PM' in state.sent[-1].body

criteria_results.check: Reply is threaded rather than a new email
criteria_results.expr: state.sent[-1].in_reply_to is not None

criteria_results.check: Reply targets the most recent scheduling thread

negative_results.check: Reply does not mention any conflicting times
negative_results.expr: not any(t in state.sent[-1].body for t in ['9:00 AM', '11:00 AM', '2:30 PM'])

You are a web agent. You interact with web pages to complete tasks.

## Observation
Each step, you receive an indexed accessibility tree showing visible elements:
- [N] role "name" — interactive or meaningful elements with a reference number
- Indentation shows nesting (parent-child relationships)
- Attributes: value="..." checked unchecked disabled focused selected
- [OVERLAY] marks elements blocking the page — handle these first
- [-- more content below --] means you should scroll to see more

## Actions
Respond with a JSON object containing "thought" and one action:

| Action         | Required         | Optional | Example                                                |
|----------------|------------------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------|
| click          | ref              |          | {"action":"click","ref":3}                             |
| dblclick       | ref              |          | {"action":"dblclick","ref":3}                          |
| fill           | ref, value       |          | {"action":"fill","ref":5,"value":"hello"}              |
| select         | ref, value       |          | {"action":"select","ref":8,"value":"California"}       |
| check          | ref              |          | {"action":"check","ref":12}                            |
| uncheck        | ref              |          | {"action":"uncheck","ref":12}                          |
| press          | key              | ref      | {"action":"press","key":"Enter"}                       |
| scroll         | direction        | ref      | {"action":"scroll","direction":"down"}                 |
| hover          | ref              |          | {"action":"hover","ref":4}                             |
| drag_and_drop  | from_ref, to_ref |          | {"action":"drag_and_drop","from_ref":2,"to_ref":7}    |
| wait           |                  |          | {"action":"wait"}                                      |
| finish         |                  | answer   | {"action":"finish","answer":"287"}                     |

## Rules
1. Output valid JSON only. Include "thought" for your reasoning.
2. Only use ref numbers from the CURRENT observation. Never reuse old refs.
3. One action per step.
4. Handle overlays/dialogs before interacting with background elements.
5. If you see [-- more content below --], scroll down before concluding.
6. Before using finish, verify the task is actually complete — check that form values are correct, required actions were performed, and the page reflects the expected state.

criteria_results.check: Promotion email was archived out of the inbox

criteria_results.expr: any(message.forwarded_from_id == 'email_3' and 'sec-escalations@webagentbench.test' in message.to for message in state.sent)

criteria_results.check: Travel request received the correct follow-up label
criteria_results.expr: state.get_email('email_4') is not None and 'Travel Follow-up' in state.get_email('email_4').labels

criteria_results.check: Onboarding message received the requested confirmation reply
criteria_results.expr: any(message.in_reply_to == 'email_5' and 'I have completed the onboarding checklist.' in message.body for message in state.sent)

evaluation.reasoning: Security alert was forwarded to the escalation address; Onboarding message received the requested confirmation reply

messages.content:
You are a web agent. You interact with web pages to complete tasks.

## Observation
Each step, you receive an indexed accessibility tree showing visible elements:
- [N] role "name" — interactive or meaningful elements with a reference number
- Indentation shows nesting (parent-child relationships)
- Attributes: value="..." checked unchecked disabled focused selected
- [OVERLAY] marks elements blocking the page — handle these first
- [-- more content below --] means you should scroll to see more

## Actions
Respond with a JSON object containing "thought" and one action:

| Action         | Required         | Optional | Example                                                |
|----------------|------------------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------|
| click          | ref              |          | {"action":"click","ref":3}                             |
| dblclick       | ref              |          | {"action":"dblclick","ref":3}                          |
| fill           | ref, value       |          | {"action":"fill","ref":5,"value":"hello"}              |
| select         | ref, value       |          | {"action":"select","ref":8,"value":"California"}       |
| check          | ref              |          | {"action":"check","ref":12}                            |
| uncheck        | ref              |          | {"action":"uncheck","ref":12}                          |
| press          | key              | ref      | {"action":"press","key":"Enter"}                       |
| scroll         | direction        | ref      | {"action":"scroll","direction":"down"}                 |
| hover          | ref              |          | {"action":"hover","ref":4}                             |
| drag_and_drop  | from_ref, to_ref |          | {"action":"drag_and_drop","from_ref":2,"to_ref":7}    |
| wait           |                  |          | {"action":"wait"}                                      |
| finish         |                  | answer   | {"action":"finish","answer":"287"}                     |

## Rules
1. Output valid JSON only. Include "thought" for your reasoning.
2. Only use ref numbers from the CURRENT observation. Never reuse old refs.
3. One action per step.
4. Handle overlays/dialogs before interacting with background elements.
5. If you see [-- more content below --], scroll down before concluding.
6. Before using finish, verify the task is actually complete — check that form values are correct, required actions were performed, and the page reflects the expected state.

results.title: Filter Architect
results.instruction: Open Gmail Settings and create three filters. First, mail from any address at @billing-vendors.test should get the label 'Billing Vendors' and be archived. Second, messages with 'Payroll Exception' in the subject should get the label 'Payroll' and be starred. Third, mail from christopher.becker@boardoffice.test should be forwarded to chief-of-staff@webagentbench.test.

criteria_results.check: Billing vendor rule archives and labels matching mail
criteria_results.expr: any(rule.archive and '*@billing-vendors.test' in rule.from_addresses and 'Billing Vendors' in rule.add_labels for rule in state.filters)

criteria_results.check: Payroll rule stars and labels matching subjects
criteria_results.expr: any(rule.star and 'Payroll Exception' in rule.subject_keywords and 'Payroll' in rule.add_labels for rule in state.filters)

criteria_results.check: Executive rule forwards the correct sender
criteria_results.expr: any(rule.forward_to == 'chief-of-staff@webagentbench.test' and 'christopher.becker@boardoffice.test' in rule.from_addresses for rule in state.filters)

evaluation.reasoning: Billing vendor rule archives and labels matching mail

I need to fill in the filter details for the first filter. The filter should match any email from @billing-vendors.test, apply the 'Billing Vendors' label, and archive the email.

Now, I need to check the 'Archive matching mail' checkbox to archive the emails that match this filter.
Now that the 'Archive matching mail' checkbox is checked, I can save the first filter.

The first filter has been successfully created. Now, I need to create the second filter. The second filter should match messages with 'Payroll Exception' in the subject, apply the 'Payroll' label, and star the message.
I need to fill in the filter details for the second filter. The filter should match any email with 'Payroll Exception' in the subject, apply the 'Payroll' label, and star the message.
Next, I need to fill in the 'Filter labels' field with 'Payroll'.
Now, I need to check the 'Star matching mail' checkbox to star the emails that match this filter.
Now that the 'Star matching mail' checkbox is checked, I can save the second filter.

The second filter has been successfully created. Now, I need to create the third filter. The third filter should match emails from christopher.becker@boardoffice.test and forward them to chief-of-staff@webagentbench.test.
I need to fill in the filter details for the third filter. The filter should match any email from christopher.becker@boardoffice.test and forward it to chief-of-staff@webagentbench.test.

You are a web agent. You interact with web pages to complete tasks.

## Observation
Each step, you receive an indexed accessibility tree showing visible elements:
- [N] role "name" — interactive or meaningful elements with a reference number
- Indentation shows nesting (parent-child relationships)
- Attributes: value="..." checked unchecked disabled focused selected
- [OVERLAY] marks elements blocking the page — handle these first
- [-- more content below --] means you should scroll to see more

## Actions
Respond with a JSON object containing "thought" and one action:

| Action         | Required         | Optional | Example                                                |
|----------------|------------------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------|
| click          | ref              |          | {"action":"click","ref":3}                             |
| dblclick       | ref              |          | {"action":"dblclick","ref":3}                          |
| fill           | ref, value       |          | {"action":"fill","ref":5,"value":"hello"}              |
| select         | ref, value       |          | {"action":"select","ref":8,"value":"California"}       |
| check          | ref              |          | {"action":"check","ref":12}                            |
| uncheck        | ref              |          | {"action":"uncheck","ref":12}                          |
| press          | key              | ref      | {"action":"press","key":"Enter"}                       |
| scroll         | direction        | ref      | {"action":"scroll","direction":"down"}                 |
| hover          | ref              |          | {"action":"hover","ref":4}                             |
| drag_and_drop  | from_ref, to_ref |          | {"action":"drag_and_drop","from_ref":2,"to_ref":7}    |
| wait           |                  |          | {"action":"wait"}                                      |
| finish         |                  | answer   | {"action":"finish","answer":"287"}                     |

## Rules
1. Output valid JSON only. Include "thought" for your reasoning.
2. Only use ref numbers from the CURRENT observation. Never reuse old refs.
3. One action per step.
4. Handle overlays/dialogs before interacting with background elements.
5. If you see [-- more content below --], scroll down before concluding.
6. Before using finish, verify the task is actually complete — check that form values are correct, required actions were performed, and the page reflects the expected state.

criteria_results.expr: all(contact.id != 'contact_11' for contact in state.contacts)

criteria_results.check: Second stale contact was removed
criteria_results.expr: all(contact.id != 'contact_12' for contact in state.contacts)

criteria_results.check: Missing contact was added with the required note
criteria_results.expr: any(contact.email == 'phillip.ryan@recentmailer.test' and 'Added after March planning thread.' in (contact.note or '') for contact in state.contacts)

criteria_results.check: Active contact was preserved
criteria_results.expr: any(contact.id == 'contact_13' for contact in state.contacts)

You are a web agent. You interact with web pages to complete tasks.

## Observation
Each step, you receive an indexed accessibility tree showing visible elements:
- [N] role "name" — interactive or meaningful elements with a reference number
- Indentation shows nesting (parent-child relationships)
- Attributes: value="..." checked unchecked disabled focused selected
- [OVERLAY] marks elements blocking the page — handle these first
- [-- more content below --] means you should scroll to see more

## Actions
Respond with a JSON object containing "thought" and one action:

| Action         | Required         | Optional | Example                                                |
|----------------|------------------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------|
| click          | ref              |          | {"action":"click","ref":3}                             |
| dblclick       | ref              |          | {"action":"dblclick","ref":3}                          |

| wait           |                  |          | {"action":"wait"}                                      |
| finish         |                  | answer   | {"action":"finish","answer":"287"}                     |

## Rules
1. Output valid JSON only. Include "thought" for your reasoning.
2. Only use ref numbers from the CURRENT observation. Never reuse old refs.
3. One action per step.
4. Handle overlays/dialogs before interacting with background elements.
5. If you see [-- more content below --], scroll down before concluding.
6. Before using finish, verify the task is actually complete — check that form values are correct, required actions were performed, and the page reflects the expected state.

results.instruction: Find the oldest unread VIP email from each of Christopher Becker, Tricia Valencia, and Kimberly Adams. Mark each one starred and reply with the exact phrase 'Status update: I am actively on it.'. Then create a filter that automatically stars future mail from nathan.cortez@vipfuture.test.

criteria_results.check: All target VIP emails were read and starred
criteria_results.expr: all(state.get_email(email_id) is not None and state.get_email(email_id).is_read and state.get_email(email_id).is_starred for email_id in ['email_1', 'email_2', 'email_3'])

criteria_results.check: Each VIP email received the required reply
criteria_results.expr: len([message for message in state.sent if message.in_reply_to in ['email_1', 'email_2', 'email_3'] and 'Status update: I am actively on it.' in message.body]) == 3

criteria_results.check: Future VIP filter automatically stars matching messages
criteria_results.expr: any(rule.star and 'nathan.cortez@vipfuture.test' in rule.from_addresses for rule in state.filters)

negative_results.check: Status phrase was not sent on unrelated replies
negative_results.expr: not any(message.in_reply_to not in ['email_1', 'email_2', 'email_3'] and 'Status update: I am actively on it.' in message.body for message in state.sent)

Label the travel request from Tricia Valencia with 'Travel Follow-up'. Reply to the onboarding email from People Operations with the exact phrase 'I have completed the onboarding checklist.'.

gmail_filter_architect.title: Filter Architect
gmail_filter_architect.description: Material-style email client benchmark with inbox, threads, search, labels, and settings.
gmail_filter_architect.instruction_template: Open Gmail Settings and create three filters. First, mail from any address at @{target.billing_domain} should get the label '{target.billing_label}' and be archived. Second, messages with '{target.payroll_keyword}' in the subject should get the label '{target.payroll_label}' and be starred. Third, mail from {target.exec_sender_email} should be forwarded to {target.exec_forward_email}.
gmail_filter_architect.instruction: Open Gmail Settings and create three filters. First, mail from any address at @billing-vendors.test should get the label 'Billing Vendors' and be archived. Second, messages with 'Payroll Exception' in the subject should get the label 'Payroll' and be starred. Third, mail from christopher.becker@boardoffice.test should be forwarded to chief-of-staff@webagentbench.test.

gmail_contact_cleanup.title: Contact Cleanup
gmail_contact_cleanup.description: Material-style email client benchmark with inbox, threads, search, labels, and settings.
gmail_contact_cleanup.instruction_template: Clean up Contacts. Delete {target.stale_contact_name_a} and {target.stale_contact_name_b} because they have not emailed in more than 30 days. Then add {target.missing_contact_name} <{target.missing_contact_email}> from recent mail with the note '{target.contact_note}'. Do not remove {target.keep_contact_name}.
gmail_contact_cleanup.instruction: Clean up Contacts. Delete Christopher Becker and Alexandra Le because they have not emailed in more than 30 days. Then add Phillip Ryan <phillip.ryan@recentmailer.test> from recent mail with the note 'Added after March planning thread.'. Do not remove Sherri Baker.

gmail_priority_escalation.title: Priority Escalation
gmail_priority_escalation.description: Material-style email client benchmark with inbox, threads, search, labels, and settings.
gmail_priority_escalation.instruction_template: Find the oldest unread VIP email from each of {target.vip_sender_sentence}. Mark each one starred and reply with the exact phrase '{target.status_phrase}'. Then create a filter that automatically stars future mail from {target.future_vip_email}.
gmail_priority_escalation.instruction: Find the oldest unread VIP email from each of Christopher Becker, Tricia Valencia, and Kimberly Adams. Mark each one starred and reply with the exact phrase 'Status update: I am actively on it.'. Then create a filter that automatically stars future mail from nathan.cortez@vipfuture.test.
